lessons.title: Why Were We Created?
lessons.subtitle: Discovering Human Purpose through Divine Revelation
objectives: Explain why the purpose of something is determined by its maker.
Explain why human intellect alone cannot determine the purpose of human existence.
Identify the correct source for answering the question, 'Why were we created?'
Explain the Islamic answer to the purpose of creation.
Support their answer using Qur'anic evidence.

sections.title: 1. The Big Questions
sections.content:
Every human being, whether young or old, eventually asks some of the most fundamental questions of existence:

• Why am I here?
• What is the purpose of my existence?
• Is there a reason behind my creation?
• What am I supposed to do in this life?

How a person answers these questions determines how they live their entire life.

callout.title: Core Principle
callout.text: The way a person answers their purpose of existence shapes every single decision, priority, and value in their life.

sections.title: 2. The Strange Tool Analogy
sections.story: Imagine cleaning out a basement and discovering a strange, unfamiliar tool. You have never seen it before. How would you find out its purpose?

options.feedback: Guessing alone can lead to misunderstanding the item's true function.

options.text: Assume it has no purpose at all
options.feedback: Intentionally designed objects always serve a purpose!

options.text: Ask the creator / read the manufacturer's manual
options.feedback: Exactly! The maker of the tool knows best why it was designed.

sections.takeaway: The value of anything is determined by its purpose. A shoe protects feet; a hammer drives nails; a key unlocks doors. If a simple tool has a purpose, how could a human being—far more complex and precious—exist without one?

sections.title: 3. The Limits of Human Intellect
sections.content:
Throughout history, great thinkers spent lifetimes trying to discover why humans exist. The famous philosopher Plato admitted: 'I neither know nor think that I know.'

Why did brilliant minds fail to agree?
sections.explanation: Human intellect is a powerful gift, but it has boundaries. A painting cannot explain why the artist painted it; a tool cannot explain why it was manufactured. Likewise, human beings cannot independently guess their ultimate purpose because they did not create themselves.
sections.solution: The Qur'an acts as the Divine Instruction Manual for human life, sent by Allah—our Creator.

verses.arabic: إِنَّمَا يَخْشَى اللَّهَ مِنْ عِبَادِهِ الْعُلَمَاءُ
verses.translation: Only those fear Allah, from among His servants, who possess knowledge.
verses.note: Affirms Allah as the Creator and knowledge as the path to reverence.

verses.arabic: الَّذِي خَلَقَ الْمَوْتَ وَالْحَيَاةَ لِيَبْلُوَكُمْ أَيُّكُمْ أَحْسَنُ عَمَلًا
verses.translation: He who created death and life to test you as to which of you is best in deeds.
verses.note: Life is an examination ground to show who performs the best deeds.

verses.arabic: وَمَا خَلَقْتُ الْجِنَّ وَالْإِنسَ إِلَّا لِيَعْبُدُونِ
verses.translation: And I did not create the jinn and mankind except that they worship Me.
verses.note: Direct declaration of the primary purpose of human existence.

sections.title: 5. What Does Worship ('Ibadah) Really Mean?
sections.intro: Worship is not limited to ritual prayers or fasting. In Islam, 'Ibadah encompasses every noble action done sincerely for the sake of Allah.

quiz.question: Who is best qualified to define the purpose of an object or creation?
options: Anyone who happens to find it
The creator / manufacturer of that object
It has no purpose until someone invents one
quiz.explanation: Just as the manufacturer knows why a tool was made, Allah—the Creator—defines the purpose of human existence.

quiz.question: Why did famous philosophers like Plato struggle to agree on human purpose?
options: They relied solely on human intellect without seeking Divine Revelation
They did not care about the question
They did not have modern computers
quiz.explanation: Human intellect has limits. A created entity cannot determine its purpose without reference to its Creator.

quiz.question: According to Surah Adh-Dhariyat (51:56), why were jinn and mankind created?
options: To compete with one another in power
quiz.explanation: Allah states clearly: 'And I did not create the jinn and mankind except that they worship Me.'

quiz.question: Which of the following describes the complete Islamic understanding of worship ('Ibadah)?
options: Every righteous deed done sincerely for Allah (prayers, honesty, helping others, seeking knowledge)
quiz.explanation: 'Ibadah in Islam covers rituals, good manners, honesty, helping others, and seeking knowledge when done for Allah.

lessons.title: Knowing Allah Through His Names & Attributes
lessons.subtitle: Understanding Asma wa Sifat and How They Transform the Heart
objectives: Understand why knowing Allah's Names & Attributes is foundational to Tawheed.
Differentiate between Ruboobiyyah, Uloohiyyah, and Asma wa Sifat.
Explain how knowing Allah as Ar-Rahman and Al-Alim impacts daily character.

sections.title: 1. Why Do We Study Asma wa Sifat?
sections.content: You cannot truly love or worship someone you do not know. Studying Allah's Most Beautiful Names (Al-Asma al-Husna) allows a believer to build a deep, intimate, and reverent connection with their Creator.

callout.title: Prophetic Principle
callout.text: The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Allah has ninety-nine names, one hundred minus one. Whoever learns and acts upon them will enter Paradise.' (Sahih Al-Bukhari)

sections.title: 2. The Three Dimensions of Tawheed
sections.intro: Scholars categorized Tawheed into three interconnected dimensions to make it clear and easy to study:

categories.name: Tawheed ar-Ruboobiyyah
examples: Allah alone creates, owns, and manages the universe.

categories.name: Tawheed al-Uloohiyyah
examples: All acts of worship belong to Allah alone without partners.

categories.name: Tawheed al-Asma wa Sifat
examples: Affirming Allah's perfect attributes as described in revelation.

quiz.question: Which dimension of Tawheed refers to Allah alone being the Creator and Sustainer of the universe?
quiz.explanation: Tawheed ar-Ruboobiyyah refers to Allah's Lordship—creating, maintaining, and controlling everything.

lessons.title: The Natural Disposition (Fitrah) & Iman
lessons.subtitle: How Faith Is Inherent in Human Nature
objectives: Define the concept of Fitrah in Islamic theology.
Explain how outer influences can veil or nurture the natural disposition.

lessons.title: Tawheed in Daily Life
lessons.subtitle: Living Sincere Faith in Modern Times
objectives: Apply Tawheed to modern daily challenges and social media pressures.

flashcards.definition: The innate, natural inclination within every human being to recognize and worship One Creator.
An all-inclusive term for every word, action, and intention loved and accepted by Allah.
The Oneness of Allah in His Lordship—creating, sustaining, and managing all creation.
The Oneness of Allah in Worship—directing all prayers, hopes, and devotion to Him alone.
The Oneness of Allah in His Divine Names and Attributes without likeness or distortion.
Wisdom; placing things in their proper place in accordance with divine guidance.

badges.desc: Completed Lesson 1 Mission: Why Were We Created?
